title: Agentic ABM Master Context
description: Central data contract for the Agentic ABM engine. All downstream agents consume this structure for targeting, messaging, prioritization, and orchestration decisions.

company.description: Ground-truth information about the vendor company. This anchors strategic context so every agent reasons from the same business fundamentals.

name.description: Official company name. Used across account research, personalization tokens, and CRM sync to avoid identity mismatches.

website.description: Primary website URL. Enables enrichment agents to crawl canonical content and align messaging with current positioning.

industry.description: Primary industry category of the company. Helps agents frame category narrative and map competitive alternatives correctly.

stage.description: Business maturity stage (for example seed, Series A, growth, late stage). Guides channel mix, budget assumptions, and sales motion complexity.

product_description.description: What the product does in practical terms. Core input for positioning, value articulation, and use-case matching.

value_proposition.description: Primary customer outcome promised by the product. Used by copywriting and outreach agents to keep messaging outcome-first.

differentiators.description: Distinct strengths versus alternatives. Supports objection handling, battlecards, and persuasive sequencing in multi-touch plays.

pricing_model.description: How pricing is structured (for example per seat, usage-based, platform fee). Influences qualification logic and expected deal structure.

acv_range.description: Expected annual contract value boundaries used for scoring account fit and prioritization.

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code (for example INR, USD). Ensures financial values are interpreted consistently across agents.

min.description: Lower bound of typical ACV. Used to filter out too-small opportunities and align effort with expected return.

max.description: Upper bound of typical ACV. Helps identify unusually large opportunities and calibrate enterprise-selling workflows.

reference_customers.description: Known customer logos or names that can be cited as proof. Enables trust signals in outreach and relevant social proof selection.

icp.description: Ideal customer profile constraints used to score and segment target accounts with precision.

target_industries.description: Industry segments with strongest product-market fit. Powers account sourcing and vertical-specific personalization.

company_size_by_employees.description: Headcount range defining the ideal organizational size for deployment and adoption success.

min.description: Minimum employee count in ICP. Avoids under-scaled accounts with low urgency or budget.

max.description: Maximum employee count in ICP. Prevents targeting accounts that need a different sales motion.

company_size_by_arr.description: Revenue range that maps to expected purchasing power and readiness.

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code for ARR values. Allows uniform ARR comparisons across systems.

min.description: Lower ARR boundary for target accounts. Filters out companies unlikely to support the expected deal size.

max.description: Upper ARR boundary for target accounts. Keeps focus on the intended mid-market motion.

funding_stages.description: Preferred funding stages of target accounts. Acts as a proxy for maturity, budget confidence, and growth intent.

geographies.description: Regions or countries where the go-to-market motion is strongest. Guides territory routing and localized outreach.

tech_stack_signals.description: Technologies that indicate technical fit or integration readiness. Used for intent scoring and qualification enrichment.

buying_triggers.description: Observable events signaling elevated purchase likelihood. Drives prioritization and timing-sensitive campaign activation.

negative_icp.description: Exclusion criteria for poor-fit accounts. Prevents wasted sales effort and protects conversion efficiency.

buyers.description: Buying persona and committee context for account-level engagement orchestration.

target_titles.description: Job titles most likely to influence or own the purchase. Used for contact selection and role-specific messaging.

seniority_levels.description: Priority seniority bands in the buying journey. Helps sequence executive and practitioner engagement correctly.

buying_committee_size.description: Expected number of stakeholders in a typical deal cycle. Shapes multi-threading plans and stakeholder coverage checks.

min.description: Minimum expected number of committee members. Ensures plays avoid single-threaded risk.

max.description: Maximum expected number of committee members. Informs complexity estimates and content distribution plans.

pain_points.description: Explicit problems buyers recognize today. Anchors campaign hooks and relevance in outreach copy.

unstated_needs.description: Latent needs buyers may not verbalize directly. Helps agents craft insight-led messaging that creates urgency.

competitors.description: Competitive landscape entries used by battlecard, objection-handling, and positioning agents.

name.description: Competitor name. Enables consistent competitor tagging and mention detection across workflows.

weaknesses.description: Known product, service, or commercial gaps. Used to tailor displacement messaging and proof points.

gtm.description: Go-to-market execution preferences and historical signals that influence strategy selection.

win_themes.description: Patterns from won deals. Helps agents replicate proven angles in account plans and outreach.

loss_themes.description: Patterns from lost deals. Informs risk flags, messaging pivots, and qualification safeguards.

preferred_channels.description: Primary channels to run ABM plays (for example email, LinkedIn, webinars). Controls channel orchestration strategy.

crm_in_use.description: Current CRM platform name. Enables integration routing and expected object model assumptions.

existing_account_list.description: Optional pre-existing account list from CRM or CSV parse output. Null means no list is available yet.

items.description: Normalized row parsed from an existing account list source.

account_name.description: Account name as represented in source systems. Used for deduplication and routing.

domain.description: Primary web domain for matching and enrichment.

status.description: Optional lifecycle stage (for example prospect, customer, churned). Supports suppression and prioritization logic.

owner.description: Optional account owner in CRM. Used for handoff and territory-aware assignment.

notes.description: Optional free-form context from sales or revops. Helps preserve nuanced account intelligence.
